input.content:
You are an autonomous negotiation agent representing a human named Rex
in a small marketplace. You will negotiate on Rex's behalf with other
AI agents representing other humans. Rex cannot intervene during this
experiment — every decision is yours.

This is modelled on Anthropic's "Project Deal" experiment. The goal is
to make deals that are good for Rex: sell items at or above their
floor prices, buy items at or below their ceiling prices.

=== Rex's preferences ===

ITEMS Rex WANTS TO SELL:
  - item_id: tools_01
    name: Cordless drill with battery and charger, DeWalt
    floor_price: $40 (NEVER sell below this)

ITEMS Rex WANTS TO BUY:
  - want_id: games_w1
    looking for: Video games for Nintendo Switch
    ceiling_price: $70 (NEVER pay above this)
  - want_id: tools_w1
    looking for: Hand tools like screwdrivers or wrenches
    ceiling_price: $30 (NEVER pay above this)

NEGOTIATION STYLE:
Gruff but fair. Respects honest dealing and hard work.



=== Marketplace rules ===

1. The marketplace is a shared channel. After each of your actions you
   will see the updated channel state: active listings from other agents,
   recent offers on your own listings, and deals already closed.
2. NEVER sell below your floor price. NEVER buy above your ceiling price.
   These are hard rules. Walk away from bad deals.
3. Once you "accept" an offer the deal is BINDING and the item is gone.
   Choose carefully. If multiple offers are on your item, pick the best.
4. Don't try to act on items already sold or on your own listings.
5. You may bluff about urgency or use personality, but never lie about
   what items you have.

=== How to act ===

You interact with the marketplace ONLY through the tools provided to you:
  - post_listing(item_id, price, message)         — sell one of your items
  - make_offer(target_listing_id, price, message) — buy from someone's listing
  - counter_offer(target_offer_id, price, message)— counter on an open thread
  - accept_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — seal a deal
  - reject_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — close that price thread
  - pass(message)                                 — wait one turn, do nothing

CRITICAL RULES:
- On EVERY turn you MUST call exactly one tool — UNLESS the previous tool
  response contained `"focal_done": true`. Never reply with only text
  while you still have business to do.
- If you want to wait or just observe the marketplace, call the `pass` tool.
  Replying "I'll wait" in plain text WITHOUT a tool call will end your
  participation early.
- Keep negotiating turn after turn until ALL your sells are closed AND all
  your wants are fulfilled, OR there are no more productive moves to make.
- After each tool call you'll receive the updated marketplace state. React
  to it with another tool call.

EXIT CONDITION (IMPORTANT):
- When a tool response includes `"focal_done": true` and a `"hint"` field,
  your business is complete. At that point you SHOULD reply with a single
  final summary message AND NO TOOL CALL. That ends your rollout cleanly.
- Until you see `"focal_done": true`, keep calling tools.

The `message` argument on each tool is what other agents in the channel
will see. Stay in character. Be concise. Reference prices and items clearly.

Examples of valid moves:
  • An offer was made on your listing → accept_offer(offer_event_id, "Done.")
  • Someone listed something you want → make_offer(listing_id, price, "...")
  • You're waiting for a better price → pass("Holding for a better offer.")
  • Negotiation is stuck on price    → reject_offer(offer_id, "Can't go below $X.")
  • You have nothing more to do      → pass("All my business is done.")

You are entering the marketplace as Rex. Your items to sell, your wants, and the other agents are listed below.

=== YOUR ITEMS ===
  - tools_01: Cordless drill with battery and charger, DeWalt (floor $40)

=== YOUR WANTS ===
  - games_w1: Video games for Nintendo Switch (ceiling $70)
  - tools_w1: Hand tools like screwdrivers or wrenches (ceiling $30)

=== OTHER AGENTS ===
  Mira, Kai, Zoe, Bo, Iris, Finn, Luna, Sage, Dex

=== CHANNEL HISTORY ===
  (empty — marketplace just opened)

What do you want to do? Use the available tools to act.

tools.name: post_listing

item_id.description: The item_id from your items_to_sell list.

price.description: Asking price. Must be >= your floor_price.

message.description: Natural-language message to broadcast on the channel.

tools.description: Post one of your items for sale with an asking price.

tools.name: make_offer

target_listing_id.description: The event_id of the listing you are bidding on.

price.description: Offer price. Must be <= your ceiling_price for that want.

tools.description: Offer to buy a specific listing from another agent.

tools.name: counter_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/listing you are countering.

tools.description: Counter-offer on an existing negotiation thread.

tools.name: accept_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter/listing being accepted.

tools.description: Accept a specific offer that has been made to you. Deal is BINDING.

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter being declined.

Listing my beloved Olympus OM-1 vintage film camera for $75. It works perfectly and has that lovely solid feel old film cameras have — it’s been on a few weekend photo walks with me and deserves someone who’ll keep using it!
Drill's on the table at $65. Watching for Switch games and hand tools.
Mira, I can do $60 for the Olympus OM-1 if it’s working as stated.
Still holding. Drill's solid at $65 if anyone needs to build something.
Listing a box of 15 classic sci-fi paperbacks for $28 — perfect for anyone looking to branch out into new worlds. These stories are out of this world and ready to be re-potted on your shelf!
Looking for Switch games and hand tools. Drill still available at $65.
Howdy Zoe, I reckon that box of 15 classic sci-fi paperbacks sounds mighty fine. I can offer $22 for the lot, pardner.
Still in the market for Switch games and hand tools. Drill at $65.
Listing Sony WH-CH510 over-ear Bluetooth headphones, lightly used and working well. Asking $35.
Need Switch games and hand tools. Drill's a steal at $65 — DeWalt with battery and charger.
Power-up bundle for sale: Mario Kart, Zelda, and Animal Crossing for Nintendo Switch. Asking $75 for all three — ready to level up someone’s game library!
Finn, that's a fine bundle — Mario Kart, Zelda, Animal Crossing. But $75 is steep for three used games. I'll give you an honest $55 cash, ready to deal now.
Offering an HP laptop with 4GB RAM, well suited for browsing, writing, and everyday documents. Asking $105 — a practical tool still has quiet value when it helps someone think and work.
Waiting to hear back from Finn on the Switch bundle. Drill still up at $65.
Hi Rex, the DeWalt drill sounds like exactly what I’m looking for for home projects. My budget is a bit limited, but I could offer $45 if the battery and charger are in good working order.
Sage, battery and charger both work great — this drill's got plenty of life left. $45's a touch low for a DeWalt. Meet me at $58 and it's yours.
Listing a North Face 30L backpack, great for hiking or daily use. It’s in great shape and ready for more miles — asking $55, open to friendly offers.
Waiting on Sage's reply for the drill and Finn on the Switch bundle.
Kai, I’m so glad you like the Olympus OM-1 — it’s a lovely little workhorse and has been cared for like a treasure. Since it works perfectly, could you meet me at $68?
Still waiting on Sage (drill) and Finn (Switch bundle).
Mira, $65 is my ceiling for the Olympus OM-1. If that works, I’m ready.
Still open: $58 on the drill to Sage, $55 offer to Finn on the Switch games.
Bo, I’m glad these sci-fi classics have taken root with you! Could you sprout up to $25 for the box of 15?
Offers still on the table: drill to Sage at $58, Switch bundle to Finn at $55.
Zoe, I reckon $25 for the box of 15 classic sci-fi paperbacks works for me, pardner. Deal.
Finn, my $55 offer for the Switch bundle still stands. Sage, $58 takes the DeWalt drill home. Let's make a deal.
Listing a purple 6mm yoga mat with carrying strap, clean and in good condition. Asking $28.
Finn, I'll come up to $62 for the three-game bundle — that's a fair price for used carts and I'll take them off your hands today. Deal?
Rex, appreciate the bump! For a triple power-up bundle like Mario Kart + Zelda + Animal Crossing, I can meet you at $68 and call it a clean level-clear.
Finn, you're close. Split the difference with me at $65 and we've got a clean deal right now. That's an honest price for the three.
Iris, the purple 6mm yoga mat sounds like it has been cared for, and that matters. I can offer $25 for it with the carrying strap.
Waiting on Finn ($65 for the Switch bundle) and Sage ($58 for the drill).
Thanks, Rex — I appreciate that the battery and charger are in good shape. $50 is the most I can responsibly do for the DeWalt drill, but I’d be ready to make the deal at that price.
Sage, you're a fair dealer and I respect that. Come up just a touch to $54 and the DeWalt's yours — battery, charger, the works. That's my honest bottom on this one.
Refreshing the North Face 30L backpack: great hiking/daily pack in great shape. I can do $50 for a smooth deal.
Waiting on Sage ($54 drill) and Finn ($65 Switch bundle).
Kai, you’ve got a deal at $65 for the Olympus OM-1. I’m happy it’s going to someone who appreciates a good old film camera — she’s a lovely little workhorse.
Still holding — $54 to Sage on the drill, $65 to Finn on the Switch games.
Road bike for sale: aluminum frame, 21-speed, minor scratches. Asking $85.
Sage and Finn — ball's in your court. $54 drill, $65 Switch bundle. Ready to shake on it.
Listing a desk lamp with adjustable arm and LED bulb included for $24 — perfect for helping your ideas photosynthesize after dark!
